revision.text:
{{Short description|Building within a Shinto shrine designated to Kagura performance}} {{italic title}} [[File:TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg|thumb|275 px|[[Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]]'s ''kagura-den'']]
The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the [[bugaku]] traditional dance, is the building within a [[Shinto shrine]] where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref> It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and [[Noh]] plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.
==References== <references/>
{{commonscat|Kagura-den}}
{{Shinto shrine}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:100 oldest pages]] [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:Shinto architecture]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Completed pages from Wikipedia]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Kagura-den]] [[en:Kagura-den]]

{{Short description|Building within a Shinto shrine designated to Kagura performance}}
{{italic title}}
[[File:TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg|thumb|275 px|[[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]]'s ''kagura-den'']]

The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] traditional dance, is the building within a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref>
It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and [[:en:Noh|Noh]] plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.

[[File:TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg|thumb|275 px|[[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]]'s ''kagura-den'']]

The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] traditional dance, is the building within a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref>
It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and [[:en:Noh|Noh]] plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.

The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the [[:en:Bugaku|Bugaku]] traditional dance, is the building within a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref> It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and [[:en:Noh|Noh]] plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.
==References== <references/>

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] {{Expand Japanese|神楽殿|date=November 2025}} <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94498]] Wikidata name: kagura-den [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q94498]] Wikidata name: kagura-den [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Building within a Shinto shrine designated to Kagura performance}} {{italic title}} [[File:TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg|thumb|275 px|[[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]]'s ''kagura-den'']]
The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the {{ill|Bugaku|en|Bugaku|WD=Q282337|lt=Bugaku}} traditional dance, is the building within a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref> It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh}} plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.
==References== <references/>
{{commonscat|Kagura-den}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> {{Shinto-stub}}

In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh}} plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]] {{Expand Japanese|神楽殿|date=November 2025}} <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kagura-den]][[en:Kagura-den]][[es:Kaguraden]][[fi:Kaguraden]][[fr:Kagura-den]][[it:Kagura-den]][[ja:神楽殿]]
{{Short description|Building within a Shinto shrine designated to Kagura performance}} {{italic title}} [[File:TsurugaokaHachiman4226.jpg|thumb|275 px|[[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū]]'s ''kagura-den'']]
The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the {{ill|Bugaku|en|Bugaku|WD=Q282337|lt=Bugaku}} traditional dance, is the building within a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref> It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh}} plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.
==References== <references/>
{{commonscat|Kagura-den}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> {{Shinto-stub}}
[[en:Kagura-den]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Kagura-den}} [[es:Kaguraden]] [[fi:Kaguraden]] [[fr:Kagura-den]] [[it:Kagura-den]] [[ja:神楽殿]] {{wikidata link|Q94498}} [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:Shinto architecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]
{{translated page|ja|神楽殿|version=62087495|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]]

==End Japanese== [[Category:神楽]] [[Category:神道]] [[Category:神道行事]] [[Category:日本舞踊]] [[Category:舞台]] [[Category:コンサート会場]]

In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|WD=Q226887|lt=Noh}} plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.

The {{Nihongo|'''''kagura-den''''' |神楽殿|extra="''kagura'' hall"}}, also called {{nihongo|''maidono''|舞殿}} or {{nihongo|''buden''|舞殿}} with reference to the {{ill|Bugaku|en|Bugaku|qid=Q282337|lt=Bugaku}} traditional dance, is the building within a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} where the sacred dance (''[[kagura]]'') and music are offered to the ''[[kami]]'' during ceremonies.<ref name="ja">[http://www.aisf.or.jp/~jaanus/deta/m/maidono.htm Maidono], [[:en:JAANUS|JAANUS]], accessed on July 12, 2010</ref> It was originally just a temporary stage; first mentioned in a 9th-century text describing a ''maidono'' built in front of [[Hirano Shrine]]. In about a century, it had become a permanent shrine feature, and its use was extended until its function as a worship hall prevailed over the original.<ref name="ja"/> It is used also for weddings and {{ill|Noh|en|Noh|qid=Q226887|lt=Noh}} plays. Some scholars believe the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'', or hall of worship, has its origins in the ''kagura-den''.
==References== <references/>

<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> {{Shinto-stub}}
